title: 谋杀启事
summary:
Letitia Blacklock informs Philippa that Patrick and Julia are merely distant relatives with no right to make demands. Letitia claims to have fallen in love with Philippa and the boy, suggesting her financial situation might improve if she takes certain "precautions" to avoid dying. Following this conversation, Julia confronts Philippa, accusing her of being dissatisfied and hungry for money. Julia warns Philippa that she will be the prime suspect if Letitia is killed, noting that Philippa's previous comments about killing Letitia before she could die imply knowledge of a relative on their deathbed in Scotland.

Meanwhile, Inspector Corrado travels back to Milchester while plagued by nightmares of a bloodied Letitia Blacklock reproaching him for failing to save her. Upon arrival, Corrado meets with Ridestead to discuss the case. Ridestead notes that Miss Marple confirmed Letitia had not seen Patrick and Julia until two months ago. While their ages match, investigations suggest they have no connection to the case; Patrick’s naval records are genuine, and a woman claiming to be Mrs. Simmons asserts her children live with Letitia in Chipping Cleghorn.

The investigation also reviews Fletcher’s search of the mansion, noting the house was often left unlocked when Letitia and Miss Bonner went blackberry picking. Several individuals had access during these times: Miss Mulgrew, who appeared flustered; Mrs. Sweetnam, who collected beef; Miss Inchcliff, who was seen leaving a side door despite claiming she had not visited; Mrs. Eastbrook, who waited inside while walking her dog; and the Colonel, who visited with a book. Authorities plan to verify the identity of Colonel Eastbrook through British authorities in the Far East.
